33.reason: Array index out of bounds when i = n3-1, accessing c[i+1] which is beyond array

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  Array index out of bounds when i = n3-1, accessing c[i+1] which is beyond array
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  Array index out of bounds when i = n3-1, accessing c[n3] which is invalid
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  Out-of-bounds access when i == n3-1 (c[i+1] does not exist); also only merges adjacent duplicates once (fails for three or more same powers), and condition c[i].pow!=0 incorrectly skips merging constant terms (exponent 0).
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Out-of-bounds access when i == n3-1 (c[i+1] is beyond array). Also condition `c[i].pow!=0` incorrectly skips merging for exponent 0. Merging logic only handles two consecutive duplicates, fails for three or more.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The condition c[i].pow!=0 incorrectly skips merging constant terms (exponent 0). Also, single-pass adjacent merge fails to correctly combine more than two terms with the same exponent.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The condition c[i].pow!=0 incorrectly skips combining constant terms. Also, this single-pass logic fails to correctly merge more than two terms with the same exponent.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Accesses c[i+1] when i == n3-1, causing out-of-bounds read; also fails to merge more than two terms with same exponent]

8.reason: Array size 2000 may be too small for product (maximum possible terms = n1*n2, could be large). Use larger dynamic allocation or increase size.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  Array size for c (2000) may be insufficient if both input polynomials have many terms (e.g., 1000 each), causing buffer overflow.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Array size 2000 may be too small for product (maximum possible terms = n1*n2, could be large). Use larger dynamic allocation or increase size.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Array c is too small; the product of two polynomials can have up to n1*n2 terms, which may exceed 2000.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Array c is too small. It should accommodate up to n1*n2 terms, e.g., c[10005].

15.reason: The reading loop does not handle trailing spaces before a newline; if there is a space after the last term, scanf will attempt to read an additional term and fail, causing undefined behavior.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  The reading loop does not handle trailing spaces before a newline; if there is a space after the last term, scanf will attempt to read an additional term and fail, causing undefined behavior.

35.reason: Setting coefficient to 0 does not properly remove term; after sorting, zeros may interfere with output.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Setting coefficient to 0 does not properly remove term; after sorting, zeros may interfere with output.

32.reason: Loop bound i<n3 causes out-of-bounds access c[i+1] when i=n3-1. Should be i<n3-1.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Loop bound i<n3 causes out-of-bounds access c[i+1] when i=n3-1. Should be i<n3-1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Loop bound should be i < n3 - 1 to avoid out-of-bounds access to c[i+1] when i = n3 - 1.
